content: Multi-tenant isolation for WebSocket relay systems requires enforcement at three layers: (1) Connection layer — every WebSocket connection must be tagged with tenantId at handshake time (validated JWT claim); the relay must never forward messages across tenant boundaries. (2) Data layer — shared DB with tenantId column + Row Level Security or separate schemas per tenant. (3) API layer — all queries must be scoped by tenantId from authenticated session context, never from URL params. The relay's routing table (deviceId → connection) must be keyed as (tenantId, deviceId) not just deviceId to prevent cross-tenant connection hijacking.
